steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence is anchored in the past: Zewail devised his technique "in 1987." The next sentence says "Years later, he ______" — describing an event that came after 1987, viewed from that past reference point. The verb must express future-relative-to-past.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'had been honored': past perfect signals that the honoring happened before the 1987 reference point — the opposite of what "years later" requires. ✗
  • Choice B — 'would be honored': the past-future construction ("would + infinitive") correctly expresses an event that was in the future relative to 1987, told from a past-tense narrative perspective. ✓
  • Choice C — 'is honored': present tense clashes with the past-tense frame of the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'will be honored': simple future is used for events future relative to now, not relative to a past point — inconsistent with the past-tense narrative. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
